L1.area: Configuration object management forms and their database interaction logic

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in a configuration management form where a user-supplied identifier is directly concatenated into a SQL query string without parameterization, allowing a remote attacker to manipulate the database query.

L3.area: Contact groups configuration form and its database query logic
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the contact groups configuration form where the group identifier parameter is directly concatenated into a SQL SELECT query string without sanitization or parameterization, allowing remote attackers to manipulate the database query.

ground_truth.reason: The contact group identifier (cg_id) is directly concatenated into a SQL query string without sanitization or parameterization, allowing remote attackers to inject arbitrary SQL.
